items.headline: Major $1M+ reroof project underway at 871 N 15th Street
items.summary: A substantial $1.017 million roofing renovation is approved for a North San José property. The project size suggests a significant building or complex structure repair.

items.headline: Cupertino launches business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: The city is offering a business license amnesty program and launching outreach to shopping centers, likely to help unlicensed businesses comply with regulations. This could affect local retail operations and the city's tax revenue.

items.headline: New economic development incentives for retail and startups announced
items.summary: Cupertino is introducing incentives to attract and support retail businesses and startups. This represents an effort to boost the local economy and could influence which new shops and companies choose to locate in the city.

items.headline: Capitol Dental Office opens at 585 N Capitol Avenue
items.summary: A new dental office is opening in San José with a $500,000 investment. This represents a significant new business serving the Capitol area community.

items.headline: Community development funding applications being reviewed
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission will review applications for HOME and Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding on March 25. These federal grants typically support affordable housing, homeless services, and community development projects in Sunnyvale.

items.headline: Palo Alto updates zoning and building rules for retail business health
items.summary: The Planning Commission is considering amendments to zoning and building regulations designed to support retail vitality across the city. This could affect where retail businesses can operate and what rules apply to them.

items.headline: San Antonio Road area planning study examines land use and transportation
items.summary: The Planning Commission is studying potential changes to how the San Antonio Road area is zoned and how transportation flows through it. This could lead to changes in what can be built and how people move through that part of Palo Alto.

items.headline: Mixed-use housing development approved at 490 East Middlefield Road
items.summary: Mountain View City Council approved a development agreement for a mixed-use residential project on East Middlefield Road. This project will add new housing to the area and underwent a second reading before approval.
